summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: Fiserv prunes non-core assets
articles.description: The payments processor and banking tech provider has divested its education and ATM businesses this month, with more sales possible.

articles.title: AI-fueled scams drive fraud: Visa
articles.description: Cybercriminals are using artificial intelligence tools for larger attacks that target individual victims, the card network said in a fraud report.

articles.title: Trump order seeks to open Fed rails
articles.description: The administration mandated a review of how the Federal Reserve and federal agencies can aid fintech firms through more central bank access.

articles.title: Cash App will be financial ‘protector,’ Dorsey says
articles.description: As Block reorients around artificial intelligence, the app will become more interactive and predictive, co-founder Jack Dorsey said Tuesday.

articles.title: Where stablecoin use is growing
articles.description: Cross-border transactions, worker payments and intercompany settlements are emerging use cases for the digital asset, according to two reports.

articles.title: Consumers cling to cash
articles.description: Debit and credit cards have surpassed cash as the most common payment method, but consumers still rely on legal tender, a Fed report found.

articles.title: Facebook fraud becomes lightning rod
articles.description: Banks and consumer advocates are taking social media to task, while Facebook tries to rein in fake profiles and scam ads.

articles.title: Judge fines payments processor Cliq
articles.description: A federal court assessed a $6.5 million penalty over non-compliance in a 2015 Federal Trade Commission settlement but declined to appoint a receiver.
